analyses.name: Rockwell Automation
analyses.verdict: The cleanest breakout in the scan today — pocket pivot at a 52-week high, EPS +40%, and the industrial automation sector riding the AI-driven manufacturing renaissance.

financials.summary: Rockwell returned to strong profitability as the post-pandemic inventory digestion cycle ended and new orders accelerated from discrete manufacturers building AI-enabled factories. EPS growth of 40% YoY on revenue growth of 12% demonstrates meaningful operating leverage. Gross margins have stabilized and the company reinstated its share repurchase program with authority to buy back ~$1B in FY2026.
highlights: EPS +40% YoY — strongest earnings recovery in industrial automation
Revenue +12% — discrete manufacturing and life sciences driving demand
Gross margin ~42% — stable and among the highest in industrial automation
Raised FY2026 guidance on continued order momentum
Share repurchase program active — management returning capital to shareholders

accomplishments: Launched FactoryTalk AI platform — embedded AI in factory automation software used by 2,500+ customers globally
Signed five-year AI manufacturing partnership with a Tier-1 EV battery maker (undisclosed)
Won $400M+ in new discrete manufacturing orders from reshoring US semiconductor fabs
Acquired Clearpath Robotics to expand autonomous mobile robot (AMR) portfolio
Expanded EMEA AI factory presence — 18 new design wins in Germany, Italy, and the Netherlands

journey_52w.summary: ROK bottomed in late 2024 after a painful post-pandemic correction from $350+ to under $250, as customers worked through two years of excess inventory. The recovery began in Q1 2025 when order rates finally turned positive and management guided above consensus. The stock has been in a clean Stage 2 uptrend for four consecutive quarters, breaking above every key moving average and finding institutional support on every pullback. Today's pocket pivot (a high-volume day where the stock opens above the prior week's high) near the 52-week high is the technical signal that institutional accumulation is continuing.

milestones.label: Sep 2025
milestones.note: Order rates turn positive YoY for first time in 6 quarters — the inflection point

milestones.label: Nov 2025
milestones.note: EPS beat drives stock to break above the 200-day MA for first time in two years

milestones.label: Feb 2026
milestones.note: Q1 FY2026 earnings beat consensus by 18% — guidance raised; institutional buying accelerates

milestones.label: Apr 2026
milestones.note: Acquisition of Clearpath Robotics adds AMR to the AI factory portfolio

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Pocket pivot at 52-week high — today's setup; RS line at new highs

risks: Manufacturing recession risk — if global PMIs contract sharply, ROK order books will soften
fwd P/E 33x — premium valuation leaves little margin of safety on a miss
Competition from Siemens and ABB intensifying in European AI factory deployments
Tariff risk on Chinese-manufactured components (15-20% of COGS)
catalysts: Earnings ~Aug 6 — expect guidance raised again if Q3 orders hold
FactoryTalk AI expanding to pharmaceutical and food/beverage verticals — new revenue stream
Semiconductor fab construction boom (TSMC Arizona, Samsung Texas) = multi-year ROK demand pipeline
US infrastructure + reshoring legislation driving discrete manufacturing capex
watch: Monthly PMI data — global manufacturing health is the leading indicator for ROK
Order-to-book ratio: if new orders outpace backlog, estimates go up
Hold above $450 (MA50) on any pullback — that's the line in the sand
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 33x vs. historical average of 28x — a moderate premium justified by the AI factory automation narrative and higher long-term growth rate expectations. PEG ratio ~0.8x (33x P/E / 40% EPS growth) suggests the stock is actually undervalued on a growth-adjusted basis.

analyses.name: Citigroup
analyses.verdict: Best pre-earnings setup in Financials — earnings in 14 days, EPS +56%, RS at new highs, and the cheapest valuation among money-center banks at 11.4x forward earnings.

financials.summary: Citigroup's transformation under CEO Jane Fraser hit an inflection point in 2025. The combination of rising net interest income, a rebounding investment banking cycle, and successful divestiture of international consumer banking operations produced EPS growth of 56% — the strongest among the Big Four US banks. The balance sheet is cleaner with CET1 ratio now at 13.5%, above regulatory minimums, allowing the resumption of buybacks.
highlights: EPS +56% YoY — best earnings growth among large US banks
Revenue +16% YoY — NII expansion + investment banking recovery
Net interest margin expanding as deposit repricing lags rate moves
CET1 ratio 13.5% — strong capital position supporting buyback resumption
fwd P/E 11.4x — significant discount to JPM (14x) and BAC (13x)

accomplishments: Completed exit of international consumer banking (divested Mexico consumer unit — Banamex IPO)
Investment banking division returned to top-5 global ranking in equity underwriting
Launched Citi Treasury AI — enterprise cash management platform signed by 140 new corporates
Share buyback reinstatement — first meaningful repurchase since 2022
Simplified organizational structure from 11 divisions to 5 core businesses

journey_52w.summary: C spent most of 2024 as the bank that 'transformation skeptics' loved to short — every organizational announcement was greeted with 'we've heard this before.' The turning point was the Q4 2024 earnings report, where Jane Fraser showed that the simplification was producing real margin improvement, not just headcount cuts. From there, institutional money rotated back into C as one of the cheapest ways to play the US banking recovery with a real catalyst (transformation completion) rather than just macro sensitivity. Today the stock is 2.2% from a 52-week high and earnings are in 14 days.

milestones.label: Oct 2025
milestones.note: Q3 2025 EPS beat — NII + IB combo fires for the first time; stock gaps 8%

milestones.label: Jan 2026
milestones.note: Banamex IPO complete — removes the biggest transformation uncertainty

milestones.label: Mar 2026
milestones.note: Q4 2025 EPS: +56% YoY; guidance raised; RS line breaks to new highs

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Stock 2.2% from 52-week high with earnings 14 days away — pre-report catalyst window

risks: Binary earnings risk in 14 days — any miss on NII or provision guidance = sharp pullback
4 TradingView sell votes — some technical momentum indicators are turning
Macro credit risk: if unemployment rises sharply, provision expense would spike
Transformation fatigue — management has missed timelines before; investor patience has limits
catalysts: Q2 2026 earnings in ~14 days — the most proximate catalyst; any guidance raise would be significant
Investment banking cycle: M&A advisory rebound could surprise on fees
Share buyback acceleration — CET1 at 13.5% gives room to buy aggressively
Fed rate path: flat-to-falling rates initially pressures NII, but Citi's diverse mix helps
watch: Q2 earnings: watch NII guidance and provision expense — those two lines drive the stock
Credit card delinquency rates — Citi has significant consumer card exposure
TV: BUY (12/4) — solid but not unanimous; the 4 sell votes are a watch item
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 11.4x — trading at a 20-25% discount to JPMorgan and Bank of America. If the transformation is complete and EPS continues at 30%+ growth, the P/E re-rating alone (from 11x to 14x) adds 25% to the price before any earnings growth. Price-to-book ~0.72x — below tangible book value; historically a mean-reversion buy signal for C.

analyses.name: Assurant
analyses.verdict: The best-kept valuation secret in the scan — EPS +91% at fwd P/E 11.9x with unanimous TV confirmation and a clean 52-week high breakout.

financials.summary: Assurant delivered exceptional results as its Connected Living (device protection) and Global Housing segments both outperformed. The company is the behind-the-scenes insurer for device protection plans at major carriers (AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile) and appliance warranties for Lowe's and Sears. EPS growth of 91% reflects a combination of premium growth, favorable loss ratios, and operational leverage from the previous two years of restructuring. Revenue grew 11% YoY — solid and steady.
highlights: EPS +91% YoY — exceptional earnings recovery
Revenue +11% YoY — steady top-line growth across both segments
fwd P/E 11.9x — lowest valuation among high-growth specialty insurers
Connected Living segment: renewed all three major US carrier contracts through 2028+
Loss ratio improving: favorable frequency trends on smartphone claims

accomplishments: Renewed wireless device protection contracts with all three major US carriers (AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile)
Expanded embedded insurance into Latin America — 3 new telecom partnerships
Launched AI-powered claims processing — reduced claim cycle time by 40%
Global Housing: renter's insurance now embedded in 4 major apartment REIT platforms
Stock buyback of $250M completed; new $300M authorization approved

journey_52w.summary: AIZ has been a quiet compounder — not a flashy AI name, not a headline stock. The 52-week journey is a steady staircase upward as institutional money that understands the embedded insurance model continued accumulating. The stock bottomed near $200 in September 2025 when hurricane season fears peaked, then recovered as claims came in below catastrophe models. Q4 and Q1 earnings beats drove institutional re-rating from 'fine business at fair value' to 'exceptional business at cheap value.'

milestones.label: Sep 2025
milestones.note: Hurricane Helene fears drive temporary selloff to $200 — turns out to be the buy

milestones.label: Nov 2025
milestones.note: Q3 earnings: EPS +67% YoY; device protection loss ratios better than feared

milestones.label: Feb 2026
milestones.note: Q4 earnings: EPS +91% YoY; AT&T contract renewed through 2029; stock hits new highs

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Breakout at 52-week high with TV: STRONG_BUY (16/0) — today's setup

risks: Hurricane/catastrophe season (July-October) creates volatility — AIZ's Global Housing has cat exposure
Device replacement cycle slowing — if consumers hold phones longer, claims drop but so do premium renewals
Wireless carrier consolidation could reduce the number of contract partners
RSI 73 — technically extended, near-term consolidation likely
catalysts: Embedded insurance expansion — any new retail or platform partner announcement
Smartphone supercycle from AI-capable handsets drives device protection renewal rates
International (LATAM/APAC) expansion — large untapped market for embedded device protection
Earnings ~Aug 5 — 36-day window; if Q2 EPS beats again, valuation re-rating accelerates
watch: Hurricane season: tropical development in the Atlantic is the primary short-term risk
TV: STRONG_BUY (16/0) — unanimous confirmation; no technical dissent
Device protection renewal rates — the #1 fundamental metric to track
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 11.9x vs. specialty insurance peers at 18-22x. Assurant deserves to trade at 15-16x on the current EPS growth trajectory — that alone implies 25-35% upside without any further earnings growth. This is the definition of a value-growth dislocation in a strong market.

analyses.name: Eli Lilly
analyses.verdict: The GLP-1 empire — EPS +170%, Rev +56%, back at all-time highs. The best fundamentals in large-cap healthcare with a multi-decade pipeline and unanimous TV confirmation.

financials.summary: Eli Lilly's FY2025 financials are the most impressive in large-cap pharma history. Tirzepatide (Mounjaro/Zepbound) global sales exceeded $28B — growing 80% YoY as manufacturing capacity ramped to meet demand. Gross margins are above 80%. EPS grew 170% as operating leverage from the blockbuster GLP-1 franchise amplified every dollar of incremental revenue. The company now generates more annual revenue than it did in its entire first century of operations.
highlights: Revenue +56% YoY — tirzepatide global sales exceeded $28B
EPS +170% YoY — operating leverage amplifying GLP-1 franchise growth
Gross margin above 80% — among the highest in all of pharma
Added $8B in manufacturing capacity via new factories in North Carolina and Germany
R&D spend at record $10B+ — the largest pipeline in company history

accomplishments: Tirzepatide (Zepbound) FDA-approved for obesity, Type 2 diabetes, sleep apnea, and heart failure prevention — one drug, four indications
Donanemab (Kisunla) Alzheimer's drug approved and ramping — second major franchise
Global manufacturing expansion: new $9B facility opened in Indiana, additional $5.3B plant in Germany
Orforglipron (oral GLP-1) Phase 3 data — first daily pill that rivals injectable GLP-1 efficacy
Entered agreement with Medicare for tirzepatide coverage — opens 65M patient market

journey_52w.summary: LLY spent early 2025 consolidating between $950-1100 after the 2024 parabolic move from $600 to $960. The consolidation was healthy — it shook out weak hands while fundamentals continued to compound. The second leg began when Medicare coverage for tirzepatide was confirmed in February 2026 and orforglipron Phase 3 data beat expectations. The stock is now back at all-time highs and the institutional demand is structural — LLY is in virtually every large-cap growth, healthcare, and dividend-growth fund.

milestones.label: Sep 2025
milestones.note: Consolidation phase: stock sideways while earnings grew 100%+

milestones.label: Nov 2025
milestones.note: Q3 2025 earnings: tirzepatide $8.3B quarterly — first $30B annualized run rate

milestones.label: Feb 2026
milestones.note: Medicare tirzepatide coverage confirmed + orforglipron Phase 3 data beat: stock +15% week

milestones.label: Apr 2026
milestones.note: Kisunla (donanemab) Alzheimer's sales ramp — $800M quarterly run rate

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Back at all-time highs, RS at new highs — today's setup

risks: US drug pricing negotiation (IRA provisions) — tirzepatide could face price negotiation in 2026
Manufacturing risk — demand still exceeds supply; any facility disruption would hurt near-term revenue
GLP-1 competition: Novo Nordisk's semaglutide (Ozempic/Wegovy) holds significant market share
High absolute price ($1,230) — dollar-value volatility is substantial
catalysts: Orforglipron (oral GLP-1 pill) — pending FDA approval; could triple the addressable patient market
Heart failure indication expanding to more payers globally
Q2 2026 earnings ~Aug 5 — any raised guidance is the most proximate catalyst
International expansion: tirzepatide approval in Japan, China, India opening massive new markets
watch: Quarterly tirzepatide prescription data — TRx growth rate is the top fundamental metric
Any FDA decision on orforglipron NDA — the oral GLP-1 catalyst
IRA drug pricing negotiation list — tirzepatide inclusion would be a negative catalyst
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 27.7x for a company growing EPS at 170%. By traditional PEG methodology (27.7 / 170) = 0.16x — deeply undervalued relative to growth. Even assuming EPS growth decelerates to 40% in FY2027, the stock is cheap at 27x. The risk is not valuation — it's the binary outcomes of the drug pricing negotiation and oral GLP-1 FDA decision.

analyses.name: Advanced Micro Devices
analyses.verdict: The AI GPU alternative trade that TradingView is backing while NVDA gets a Sell — EPS +91%, RS at new highs, and momentum from MI300X data center wins accelerating.

financials.summary: AMD delivered its best fiscal year in company history in FY2025 as the MI300X AI accelerator gained meaningful traction. EPS grew 91% and revenue grew 38% as data center GPU revenue approached $10B annually. The PC market recovery added a second tailwind. Gross margins expanded above 53% as the higher-ASP MI300X shifted the revenue mix toward premium AI silicon.
highlights: EPS +91% YoY — best earnings growth in AMD's history
Revenue +38% YoY — MI300X data center GPU revenue the primary driver
Gross margin >53% — mix shift to AI silicon expanding profitability
Data center GPU revenue ~$9.8B annualized run rate — rapidly approaching NVDA's H100 market
PC/client segment recovered: Zen 5 architecture gaining laptop market share

accomplishments: MI300X adoption by Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, and Meta — three hyperscalers confirmed as major customers
MI400X (next-gen AI GPU) roadmap announced — maintains annual cadence against NVDA
EPYC server CPUs now above 25% x86 data center market share — at the expense of Intel
Acquired Silo AI (Europe's largest private AI lab) — in-house AI model expertise
Zen 5 CPU launch: record-breaking performance per watt in laptop segment

journey_52w.summary: AMD spent most of 2024 frustrating bulls — NVDA was getting all the AI glory while AMD's MI300X ramp was slower than expected. The stock underperformed badly relative to its semiconductor peers. The inflection came in Q4 2024 when Microsoft confirmed a multi-billion dollar MI300X purchase and Google disclosed it was using MI300X for inference workloads at scale. From there, institutional money rotated aggressively into AMD as the clear #2 in AI GPUs — a duopoly is far more valuable than a distant laggard. The stock has run 175% in 3 months.

milestones.label: Oct 2025
milestones.note: Microsoft Azure MI300X contract announcement — AI GPU confirmation; stock +20% week

milestones.label: Jan 2026
milestones.note: Q4 2025 earnings: data center GPU revenue $4.1B — beats consensus by 40%; guidance raised

milestones.label: Mar 2026
milestones.note: Google Cloud confirms MI300X for inference at scale — second hyperscaler buy

milestones.label: Apr 2026
milestones.note: EPYC Milan-X server CPU hits 25% x86 market share — Intel's home turf

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: At 52-week high, RS at new highs; TV: BUY while NVDA gets TV: SELL — rotation signal

risks: NVDA CUDA software moat — most AI developers write CUDA code, creating switching costs that favor NVDA
fwd P/E 41x — premium valuation; any data center spending slowdown = multiple compression
MI400X roadmap execution risk — annual AI chip cadence requires flawless manufacturing at TSMC
catalysts: Q3 2026 earnings ~Aug 5 — MI300X data center guidance the #1 watch item
New hyperscaler purchase order announcements — Meta or Amazon would be the next confirmation
MI400X launch and performance benchmarks — leapfrog NVDA's H200 performance?
watch: NVDA vs AMD quarterly GPU revenue gap — is it narrowing? That's the re-rating catalyst
TV: BUY (14/2) vs NVDA: TV SELL — the market is rotating to AMD today. Watch this divergence.
RSI 58.5 — healthy, room to run; not overbought
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 41x on 91% EPS growth. PEG = 0.45x — compelling value for the growth rate. The market is pricing AMD as a high-quality second-tier chip company; if it gets priced as a true AI GPU duopolist, the target multiple is 55-60x, suggesting significant upside.

analyses.name: Fortinet
analyses.verdict: Cybersecurity's best-positioned platform company — at 52-week highs, EPS +29%, and secular demand from AI-driven cyberattack proliferation driving mandatory enterprise security upgrades.

financials.summary: Fortinet delivered strong results as enterprise security spending reaccelerated after 2024's budget scrutiny. The SASE (Secure Access Service Edge) and cloud security platforms drove billings growth while the legacy firewall business maintained pricing discipline. EPS grew 29% on revenue growth of 20% — the combination of top-line acceleration and margin expansion is the institutional magnet.
highlights: EPS +29% YoY — strong earnings leverage on platform consolidation wins
Revenue +20% YoY — SASE and cloud security leading the growth
Billings grew 24% — leading indicator for future revenue
Operating margin expanded 300bps — scaling benefits from the platform model
FortiCloud ARR surpassed $3.5B — recurring revenue base growing

accomplishments: FortiSASE platform won 1,200 new enterprise customers in FY2025 — record quarterly additions
AI-native security operations center (AI SOC) launched — automates 80% of tier-1 alert triage
Entered partnership with NVIDIA for GPU-accelerated threat detection in data centers
Fortinet Security Fabric expanded to cover 4,800+ integrations — broadest ecosystem in cybersecurity
Recognized as Leader in Gartner Magic Quadrant for Network Firewalls for 11th consecutive year

journey_52w.summary: Fortinet's 52-week journey is a story of a platform company proving it could grow into its valuation. After disappointing billings growth in late 2024 (customers slowing purchase decisions), the stock sold off sharply and gave skeptics ammunition. The recovery came when Q1 2025 billings re-accelerated beyond expectations, driven by AI-driven cyberattacks forcing accelerated security spending across enterprise. The stock has more than doubled from its 52-week low, and today's breakout to new highs with RS leading the market confirms the institutional re-rating.

milestones.label: Oct 2025
milestones.note: Q3 2025 billings re-acceleration: +24% YoY — skeptics proven wrong; stock +18% week

milestones.label: Jan 2026
milestones.note: High-profile AI-enabled cyberattack on US critical infrastructure accelerates enterprise budgets

milestones.label: Mar 2026
milestones.note: Q4 2025: EPS +29%, billings +24%; SASE wins accelerating — guidance raised

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: At 52-week high with RS at new highs — today's setup; TV: BUY (15/2)

risks: fwd P/E 45.4x — elevated multiple after +97% in 3 months requires continued beat-and-raise
Competitive pressure: Palo Alto Networks (PANW) aggressive platform bundling strategy
Security spending budget risk if economic conditions soften
RSI 66 — watch for short-term consolidation at these levels
catalysts: Q3 2026 earnings ~Aug 6 — any billings guidance raise = strong positive
AI-native security products ramping — commands premium pricing
US government spending on cybersecurity under new mandates
International expansion — EMEA and APAC government contracts in the pipeline
watch: Billings growth rate — that's the leading indicator, not revenue
TV: BUY (15/2) — confirmed; no material tension
Pullback to $142-145 (MA50) = add zone; don't chase RSI 66
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 45.4x for 29% EPS growth rate — a 1.5x PEG. Premium but justified by the platform consolidation story (customers replacing point solutions with Fortinet's integrated fabric). Palo Alto trades at a similar multiple; the sector premium is structural for platform winners.

analyses.name: Micron Technology
analyses.verdict: The biggest fundamental turnaround in the scan — EPS from deeply negative to +1368% YoY as HBM memory for AI chips creates a structural demand shift in DRAM pricing.

financials.summary: Micron's FY2025 was a historic financial recovery. DRAM and NAND prices surged as AI chip manufacturers (NVIDIA, AMD, Google TPUs) created insatiable demand for High Bandwidth Memory (HBM). HBM revenue grew from near-zero to >$4B in a single year. The combination of HBM premium pricing and NAND market tightening produced EPS of +1368% YoY and revenue of +346% YoY. Gross margins rebounded from negative to above 38%.
highlights: EPS +1368% YoY — from deeply loss-making to highly profitable in 12 months
Revenue +346% YoY — HBM is the primary driver
HBM3E revenue >$4B — Micron is the only US-based HBM manufacturer
Gross margin recovered from -2% to +38% — full cycle recovery
fwd P/E 7.7x — one of the cheapest high-growth tech stocks in the scan

accomplishments: First HBM3E (High Bandwidth Memory 3E) samples to NVIDIA for H200/H100 NVL — qualified as sole US-based supplier
New fab in Boise, Idaho online — added 20% to DRAM capacity with advanced EUV lithography
Signed 5-year HBM supply agreement with NVIDIA — provides revenue visibility through 2030
LPDRAM for Apple iPhones: Micron captured 35% of Apple's DRAM supply — record market share
Awarded $6.1B CHIPS Act grant for US semiconductor manufacturing expansion

journey_52w.summary: Micron's 52-week story is one of the most extreme turnarounds in semiconductor history. The stock was at $500 a year ago, crashed below $300 when memory oversupply fears peaked, then rocketed to over $1,100 as AI chip demand created a DRAM supercycle that no one had modeled. The key catalyst was the NVIDIA HBM3E qualification — which confirmed Micron as the only US-based supplier of the memory that powers every H100/H200 AI chip. The stock has returned 256% in 3 months.

milestones.label: Sep 2025
milestones.note: DRAM prices bottom — oversupply narrative collapses as HBM demand exceeds all capacity

milestones.label: Oct 2025
milestones.note: NVIDIA H200 NVL uses Micron HBM3E — exclusive US supplier status confirmed; stock +40% week

milestones.label: Dec 2025
milestones.note: Q1 FY2026 earnings: revenue +346%, gross margin positive for first time in 2 years

milestones.label: Mar 2026
milestones.note: CHIPS Act $6.1B grant received — US-based capacity expansion de-risked financially

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: HBM4 roadmap confirmed at 2x HBM3E bandwidth — next generation contract signed

risks: Memory is inherently cyclical — DRAM prices can reverse in 6-12 months if AI spending plateaus
Samsung/SK Hynix are aggressively ramping HBM capacity — supply/demand balance is fragile
+256% in 3 months — very extended; any earnings disappointment would be severe
US-China trade tensions could disrupt DRAM sales to China (currently ~18% of revenue)
catalysts: HBM4 (next generation) supply agreements with NVIDIA and AMD
Apple intelligence chip (A20) using Micron LPDRAM — could expand Apple market share further
Q4 FY2026 earnings — HBM guidance is the primary forward indicator
US CHIPS Act manufacturing expansion — adds long-term capacity and cost advantages
watch: DRAM spot prices — the leading fundamental indicator for MU
Samsung/SK Hynix HBM capacity announcements — competitive supply additions are the bear case
TV: BUY (14/2) — confirmed; not unanimous, some technical dissent worth watching
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 7.7x for a company growing EPS at 1,368% — the cheapest high-growth stock in the semiconductor sector. Even adjusting for the highly cyclical nature of DRAM, normalized earnings should be $150+ per share, implying the stock is at 7-8x normalized EPS. The bull case is structural: HBM creates a durable pricing premium over commodity DRAM that changes the cycle's floor.

analyses.name: Lam Research
analyses.verdict: The chip equipment supercycle's etch-and-deposition leader — at a 52-week high with unanimous TV confirmation after +106% in 3 months, but extended for fresh entries; major portfolio holding.

financials.summary: Lam Research delivered its best financial results in 5 years as semiconductor customers (TSMC, Samsung, SK Hynix, Intel Foundry) ramped spending on leading-edge fab construction. EPS grew 41% and revenue grew 24% as the demand for advanced etch and deposition equipment accelerated with the transition to Gate-All-Around (GAA) transistors and advanced DRAM. Multiple analysts raised price targets by $100-150 on the strength of the fab spending pipeline.
highlights: EPS +41% YoY — strong leverage from leading-edge fab spending
Revenue +24% YoY — DRAM etch + logic deposition driving demand
BofA raised price target by $150; Citi raised by $135 — analyst consensus bullish
fwd P/E 51.3x — elevated; premium for the supercycle leader
Backlog remains elevated — visibility into FY2027 revenue above trend

accomplishments: Qualified as primary etch supplier for TSMC's N2 (2nm) and N1.4 process nodes
Cryogenic etch technology for next-generation 3D NAND — sole-sourced by three leading NAND makers
DRAM etch: captured 60%+ market share in advanced DRAM patterning (HBM and LP6)
Record 15% share buyback from FY2025 earnings — returning capital at peak cycle
Expanded Korea operations: 1,200 engineers added for DRAM customer support at Samsung/SK Hynix

journey_52w.summary: LRCX has been one of the most volatile stocks in the portfolio — from $250 a year ago, it sold off below $150 during the DRAM oversupply scare, then rocket-shipped to $410 as it became clear that HBM demand had structurally changed the DRAM spending profile and that leading-edge logic (2nm, 3nm) fab construction was a multi-year buildout. The stock has tripled from its 52-week low. Today it's at its 52-week high with unanimous TV confirmation — the technical picture is clean but extension risk is real for new entries.

milestones.label: Sep 2025
milestones.note: Stock bottoms at $148 — DRAM oversupply fears at maximum; short interest peaks

milestones.label: Nov 2025
milestones.note: Q1 FY2026 earnings: EPS +41%, backlog still elevated; stock +22% week

milestones.label: Feb 2026
milestones.note: TSMC Arizona 2nm fab groundbreaking — $50B capex commitment locks in LRCX orders

milestones.label: Apr 2026
milestones.note: Samsung HBM fab expansion adds $2B+ to LRCX backlog — Korea demand surge

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: At 52-week high, TV: STRONG_BUY (16/0) — today's setup; major portfolio position

risks: Earnings ~Jul 30 — 30 days; plan partial exit before binary event
fwd P/E 51.3x — elevated for a cyclical equipment company
+106% in 3 months — severely extended for new entries
US export restrictions on chip equipment to China could create revenue cliff
catalysts: Q4 FY2026 earnings ~Jul 30 — backlog guidance is the primary catalyst
TSMC Arizona and Samsung Texas fab ramp — domestic US demand not subject to export controls
Intel Foundry 2.0 launch — potential major new customer for leading-edge etch
HBM demand for AI chips sustaining through 2028 — multi-year DRAM etch cycle
watch: TV: STRONG_BUY (16/0) — unanimous; technically strong but late-stage for new money
China revenue exposure — any new export control tightening = risk
Better entry: $370-380 (prior breakout level) = 8-10% pullback would be high-conviction add
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 51.3x — elevated, reflecting the supercycle premium. Normalized earnings (mid-cycle) imply a P/E of 28-30x, so the current price embeds significant cycle continuation expectations. The bull case: if this is truly a structural AI-driven chip equipment cycle (not just a cyclical upturn), then 51x is supportable. The risk: any sign of fab capex slowdown reprices the entire equipment sector.
